title: heleophylax supinus
intro: Heleophylax supinus, also known as the water celery or water parsley, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. The plant is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Heleophylax supinus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Heleophylax supinus can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or by using the plant in decoctions, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
